questions.question: Why do parallel tool calls cut wall-clock time so dramatically on fan-out workloads?
options: The model thinks faster when it emits several calls
One LLM round trip replaces several, and executor time becomes the max of the calls instead of the sum
Providers give parallel calls priority routing
Parallel calls skip schema validation
questions.explanation: Serializing three weather lookups costs three LLM round trips plus the sum of executor latencies. Parallel emission costs one round trip plus only the slowest executor — production benchmarks show 60-70% wall-clock reduction.

questions.question: When should you deliberately disable parallel tool calls?
options: When tools have ordering dependencies or one call's output feeds another's input
Whenever you use more than two tools
Always in production — parallel is only for development
When the model is smaller than 70B parameters
questions.explanation: Disable parallel when order matters (create_file then write_file), when outputs chain into inputs, or when downstream rate limits cannot absorb the fan-out. Otherwise the default-parallel behavior is a free win.

questions.question: Three parallel calls complete out of order. How does the model still line results up correctly?
options: The host must sort results back into call order before replying
Each result carries the matching call id (tool_call_id / tool_use_id / functionResponse id)
The model re-issues any call whose result looks misplaced
Results are matched by tool name
questions.explanation: Ids are the correlation mechanism — order in the reply doesn't matter for correctness as long as each result cites its id. Matching by name (as pre-Gemini-3 did) breaks when two parallel calls hit the same tool.

questions.question: While streaming, when is it safe to json.loads the accumulated arguments of a tool call?
options: After every chunk — JSON parsers handle partial input
Once the brace count balances
Only on the provider's end-of-call signal, like finish_reason "tool_calls" or content_block_stop
After a fixed 500 ms timeout
questions.explanation: Partial JSON like {"city": "Beng is invalid and raises. Brace-counting gives false positives (braces inside quoted strings), so the reliable gate is the provider's completion signal — or an incremental JSON parser for live-UX cases.

questions.question: Why did Gemini 3 add unique ids to function calls?
options: To bill each call separately
Because matching results by name broke when two parallel calls hit the same tool
To support tools written in languages other than Python
For compatibility with OpenAI's id format
questions.explanation: Gemini 2 correlated results by function name, which made two parallel calls to the same tool indistinguishable. Gemini 3's UUIDs let interleaved streamed chunks and out-of-order results correlate cleanly.
